content: I wrote something on a google doc to put here (big ramble) and it's over the nitro word count LOL. I think it'd go here cause it's in relation to the story. I'll send it in two parts. Enjoy? Or tear it apart I'll take either.

Not sure this counts because it's not lore currently in the game, just what I think would be interesting. I really like gothic literature, one of the big reasons I started playing. 

Fun fact about the Cthulhu mythos, it's somewhat similar to the SCP foundation in the fact it's made by a bunch of different people with a bunch of different stories. Lovecraft is usually thought to be the creator but even when he was alive and writing other people were contributing to the lore and such. (I find myself more drawn to the history of the mythos than the mythos itself sometimes lol)

That's like completely besides the point, I just thought people might find it interesting. What I was originally thinking of was a little while ago, before I had fully finished reading the game I made a list of different literature, mostly gothic, I'd like Morimens to maybe make characters based off of. 

The reason I made the list in the first place was because I rewatched the 2023 The Fall of the House of Usher miniseries, it's on Netflix but the og story is also amazing (in fact I reread it after watching the miniseries to write this). I'd advise reading/watching both if you have the time. Anyways, I think the unnamed narrator in the short story works well with a character like the keeper who is a self insert that still manages to keep somewhat of an identity which in a way I think is what Poe was trying to do considering how much the story relies on the fact that “you” are there and living the tragedy as it happens.

“I looked at this scene, I say, with a complete sadness of soul which was no healthy, earthly feeling. There was a coldness, a sickening of the heart, in which I could discover nothing to lighten the weight I felt. What was it, I asked myself, what was it that was so fearful, so frightening in my view of the House of Usher?”

I wrote more here before realizing I was literally just analyzing The Fall of the House of Usher so I won't include that because 1. You should go read it and 2. That's not why I was writing this. But I guess it's hard to show why I think it's a good fit without doing it. Well, without spoiling everything the story is about the fear of knowing which is basically what Cthulhu represents, being so over aware it causes you to go mad… And like everyone in this game is crazy so the Usher siblings would fit right in LOL. Also more siblings.
tbh morimens is not purely based on Lovecraft but the Lovecraft mythos, which is widely accepted as the work of multiple authors that take place in the same canon
Mhm!
so if theres other open source modern media that is in the same mythos, i dont see any reason biav couldn't adapt it too
although with the Lovecraftian aspects id leave it to venush since they know better
It feels nice to talk about lore with other people <:takenotes:1357186646294138890>
I don't think I fully understand everything, I'd like to think I do but it's very likely I've missed or forgotten things, I've been considering rereading some things...
id love talking about the lore with you!
well im more focused towards whats mentioned in-game rather than the source materials, but theres certainly regulars in here that do like making the connections
my entire Prototypes tab wouldn't exist if not for their help
I've been trying to balance looking at the source material and the stories themselves but I've been slacking. I'm always looking for more Stuff™ to cram into my brain to even out the gamer in me 😭
